title: Cytisus × watereri
taxonomy: Cytisus × watereri is a hybrid plant, which is a combination of Cytisus scoparius and Laburnum x watereri. The classification of this plant can be complex due to its hybrid nature. As a result, Cytisus × watereri can be classified as a hybrid of a broom (Cytisus scoparius) and a laburnum. This classification is based on the botanical principles of hybridization. The exact classification may vary depending on the specific context or classification system used.

intro: Cytisus × watereri, also known as Waterer's broom or Waterer's broom shrub, is a hybrid plant species within the Fabaceae family. The health benefits of Cytisus × watereri include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
